data.narrative:
Asimov is a Series B biotech company founded by Alec Nielsen, Chris Voigt, Douglas Densmore, and Raja Srinivas. The target carries a current post-money valuation of $800M against $209M raised to date. The comparable universe includes 4 private peers at the same stage and sector (Eikon Therapeutics at $3.3B, Neko Health at $1.8B, New Limit at $810M, and Profluent at $600M), supplemented by 3 public biotech comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two valuation methods triangulate to a defensible range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $1.5B. Peer-median anchoring across the four same-sector, same-stage private companies produces $1.3B, with the peer set ranging from $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 7.8x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied due to unavailable revenue data on the private target.

The two applicable methods support a triangulated valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $1.4B. This implies upside of 75% from the current $800M post-money. Key uncertainties center on revenue visibility and the wide dispersion within the peer set—a 5.5x range from Profluent's $600M floor to Eikon's $3.3B ceiling—reflecting stage-specific valuation volatility in early-stage biotech.
